revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Iwashizu-jinja]][[ja:伊和志津神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Iwashizu Shrine (Q11379248) | native_name = 伊和志津神社 | image = Iwashizujinjya.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Iwashizu Shrine (Q11379248) | coordinates = {{coord|34.800089|135.353174}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|ar|سوسانو|azb|سوسانو|bcl|Susanoo|be|Сусанаа|bg|Сусаноо|ca|Susanoo|cs|Susanoo|da|Susa-no-o|de|Susanoo|el|Σουσάνoο|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|eo|Susanoo|es|Susanoo|eu|Susanoo|fa|سوسانو (خدا)|fi|Susanoo|fr|Susanoo|hr|Susano|hu|Szuszanoo|hy|Սուսանո|id|Susanoo|it|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|ka|სუსანოო-ნო-მიკოტო|ko|스사노오|la|Susano'o|lb|Susanoo|mni|ꯁꯨꯁꯅꯨ-ꯅꯣ-ꯃꯤꯀꯣꯇꯣ|nl|Susanoo|pl|Susanoo|pt|Susanoo|ro|Susanoo|ru|Сусаноо|simple|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|sl|Susanovo|sr|Сусаноо|su|Susanoo|sv|Susanoo|ta|சுசானவோ|th|ซูซาโนโอะ|tr|Susanoo|uk|Сусаноо|vi|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|zh_min_nan|Susanoo|zh|素戔嗚尊|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} }} {{nihongo|'''Iwashizu Shrine'''|伊和志津神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|lt=Shikinai Taisha|en|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|simple|Shikinai Taisha|qid=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}} * {{ill|Q134917286|ltq=Q134917286|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q1466105|ltq=Q1466105|qid=Q1466105|1=Hirota Shrine (Osaka City)}} * {{ill|Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings|lt=Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings|qid=Q135009132|1=Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings}} * {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q135009906|ltq=Q135009906|qid=Q135009906|1=Massha}} * {{ill|Kanpei-sha|lt=Kanpei-sha|simple|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|qid=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Settsu Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Settsu Province|ja|摂津国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11498550|1=List of Shikinaisha in Settsu Province}} ** Series ordinal: 49 ** P155: {{ill|Q1466105|ltq=Q1466105|qid=Q1466105|1=Hirota Shrine (Osaka City)}} ** P155: {{ill|Q135098906|ltq=Q135098906|qid=Q135098906|1=Natsukino Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Wokatano Shrine|lt=Wokatano Shrine|qid=Q135039222|1=Wokatano Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q823967|ltq=Q823967|qid=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}} * {{ill|Settsu Province|lt=Settsu Province|ca|Província de Settsu|cs|Provincie Seccu|de|Provinz Settsu|en|Settsu Province|eo|Provinco Seccu|es|Provincia de Settsu|fa|ولایت ستسو|fr|Province de Settsu|gl|Provincia de Settsu|hak|Settsu-koet|id|Provinsi Settsu|it|Settsu (provincia)|ja|摂津国|ko|셋쓰국|lt|Seccu žemė|nl|Settsu (provincie)|pt|Província de Settsu|ru|Сэтцу (провинция)|simple|Settsu Province|sk|Seccu (provincia)|sr|Провинција Сецу|tr|Settsu (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Сеццу|zh_min_nan|Settu-kok|zh_yue|攝津國|zh|攝津國|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} * {{ill|Q11545988|ltq=Q11545988|qid=Q11545988|1=Muko district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.800089|135.353174}} Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * Iwashizujinjya.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q272993|ltq=Q272993|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20041124115937/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=51003 == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181070 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} * 181069 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|ko|종5위|zh|從五位|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|es|Kasuga-zukuri|fr|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|ru|Касуга-дзукури|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いわしづじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9140005018627 == Commons category (P373) == * Iwashizu-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039221|ltq=Q135039221|qid=Q135039221|1=Iwashitsuno Shrine}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P2868: {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宝塚市伊孑志1-4-3', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '兵庫県西宮市大社町', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Iwashizu-jinja}} [[JA:伊和志津神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11379248}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Iwashizu Shrine |Image = [[File:Iwashizujinjya.JPG|270px]]
Main worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|qid=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}{{ill|lt=Isoji|Q11379317|qid=Q11379317|1=Isoji}}1-4-3 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (Great) disputed shrine
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = One-bay shrine in {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} style |Alternate name = Iwashizu Shrine |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 23|Q2958|qid=Q2958|1=October 23}}・{{ill|lt=October 24|Q2955|qid=Q2955|lt=24th|1=October 24}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Iwashizu
Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Iwashizu Shrine''' (いわしづじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Takarazuka City|Q823967|qid=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Isoji|Q11379317|qid=Q11379317|1=Isoji}}. It is a Shikinaisha Grand Shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (a {{ill|lt=disputed shrine ranking|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=ronsha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}). It is also written as “'''Iwashizu Shrine'''.” === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} :He is the {{ill|lt=guardian deity|Q1392097|qid=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} (ujigami) of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isoji Village|1=UNKNOWN}} (いそしむら) in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, {{ill|lt=Muko District|Q11545988|qid=Q11545988|1=Muko district}}. It is believed that the ancestral deity of the Isoji no Omi clan was originally enshrined here. === History === Because it was already listed as a grand shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, the shrine must have been founded no later than the 9th century. However, the surrounding area had been a base of the {{ill|lt=Isoji no Omi|UNKNOWN|lt=Isoji}}{{ill|lt=omi|Q2574718|qid=Q2574718|1=Omi (title)}} clan since the latter half of the 8th century{{refnest|group=Notes|According to the {{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|qid=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} and the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|qid=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}, the lineage runs: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Magotenomichi-ne no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} → {{ill|lt=Narahara|Q92770386|qid=Q92770386|1=Narahara}} → {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isoji|1=UNKNOWN}} → {{ill|lt=Shigeno clan|Q11564992|qid=Q11564992|1=Shigeno clan}}, sharing a common ancestor with the {{ill|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11604953|qid=Q11604953|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}.}}, and it is possible that the Isoji no Omi founded the shrine during this period. There is also an “Iwashizu Shrine” of the same disputed ranking as a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Hirota Shrine|Q1466105|qid=Q1466105|1=Hirota Shrine (Osaka City)}} ({{ill|lt=Nishinomiya City|Q243853|qid=Q243853|1=Nishinomiya}}). During the {{ill|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|Q319531|qid=Q319531|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, it is said that {{ill|lt=Katō Kiyomasa|Q1069843|qid=Q1069843|1=Katō Kiyomasa}} kept a tiger that he brought back from Korea during the {{ill|lt=Japanese invasions of Korea (1592–1598)|Q576338|qid=Q576338|1=Imjin War}} within the shrine grounds. [http://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/sub_file/..%5Csub_file%5C04030101000000-iwa.html Takarazuka Folktales - Takarazuka City] Accessed February 26, 2019. === Shrine grounds === * Main hall – A one-bay shrine in {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} style with {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q199388|qid=Q199388|1=Wood shingle}}, believed to date from the mid-{{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}. * In 1995, as part of a project to straighten {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefectural Route 337 Namaze–Mondo-Sō Line|Q11393353|qid=Q11393353|lt=prefectural road|1=Hyogo Prefectural Road Route 337}} that bypasses the shrine, the intersecting approach road was converted into a grade-separated crossing, and buildings such as the worship hall were newly constructed. * Atago Shrine * Takarazuka Suitengū === Festivals === * April 23–24 – Spring Festival * June 30 – Great Purification * July 27–28 – Summer Festival * August 24 – Atago Festival * October 23–24 – Autumn Grand Festival * December 15 – Fire Kindling Festival === Cultural properties === ; Designated cultural properties of Takarazuka City * Main hall ; Designated preserved forest of Takarazuka City * Iwashizu Shrine precincts – approximately 3,000 tsubo === Access === * 6-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Hankyu Imazu Line|Q3832688|qid=Q3832688|1=Hankyū Imazu Line}} {{ill|lt=Sakasegawa Station|Q7402849|qid=Q7402849|1=Sakasegawa Station}} === Footnotes === === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} === References === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://iwashidu-jinja.jp/about Iwashizu Shrine Official] * [http://www.y-tohara.com/settuk-iwasizu.html Settsu Shikinaisha: Iwashizu Shrine (Takarazuka City)] {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Iwashizu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Iwashizu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:武庫郡]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takarazuka, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Takarazuka, Hyogo]]
